items.title: 'Are you sure?': First Nations voters asked about identity at polling booths

children.content: South Australia's Aboriginal Affairs Minister will consider an independent review of the state's Voice to Parliament
election, following claims some voters were queried about their racial background at polling booths, while others were forced to queue for hours.

items.title: Army reservist allegedly worked for Ukraine military without authorisation

children.content: An Australian Army reservist will face an Adelaide court after he allegedly worked for the Ukraine military as a drone operator without authorisation.

image.altText: A man with hands cuffed behind him being escorted by a police officer walking on a suburban street footpath

items.title: Middle East war causes PVC prices to skyrocket

children.content: Conflict in the Middle East has disrupted petroleum and gas supply chains, causing PVC prices to skyrocket in Australia.

image.altText: A water tank in a backyard.

items.title: Outback tourist pub had two visitors this Easter amid fuel price pinch

children.content: Just two tourists have stopped at the iconic Pink Roadhouse during the Easter weekend, as travellers reconsidered their Easter weekend plans due to rising fuel costs.

gallery.altText: A road house sign at dusk

thumbnail.altText: A road house sign at dusk

items.title: Watchdog seeks upgrade to 'harmful' youth cells to improve access to justice

children.content: South Australia's Youth Court cells do not meet "acceptable minimum standards of safety, dignity and care", resulting in child detainees increasingly appearing in court via video link, the state's youth detention watchdog says.

image.altText: A room with row of small cells with persplex walls on one side, lockers and a TV screen on the other. Two toilets in the far end

items.title: Feral animals, biosecurity and careers on agenda for young Aboriginal rangers

children.content: The first SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Ranger Conference has brought together those on the front line of the fight against feral animals and plants.

image.altText: A man holding a bag out of which a small warru black footed rock wallaby has jumped out of

items.title: 16yo surfer feels 'lucky' to have foot after SA shark attack

children.content: Oliver Tokic-Bensley says he was sitting on his surfboard alone at Middleton Beach when he said he felt something grab his foot.
Warning: This story contains images some readers may find distressing.

gallery.altText: The back of a 16-year-old boy's head as he holds his surfboard in front of him while looking out at the water.

thumbnail.altText: The back of a 16-year-old boy's head as he holds his surfboard in front of him while looking out at the water.

items.title: Man found dead after being swept away in floodwaters in SA's far north

children.content: Police have found the body of a man in his 60s who was swept away trying to cross a flooded road on foot in SA's far north over the Easter weekend.

image.altText: A man looks into the camera. He has white hair and is wearing a hat.

items.title: 'Struggling massively': Farmer finds it hard to put food on the table

children.content: Soaring fuel costs and interest rates are forcing some South Australians to change their travel plans as a small producer says he has struggled to put food on the table.

image.altText: A man stands behind a glass refrigerated counter under a marquee. A sign Lakeview Acres Farm hung behind him

items.title: 7yo girl dies after ATV rollover in Adelaide's north-east

children.content: The Easter long weekend also saw two motorcyclists lose their lives in a separate crash in the Adelaide Hills.

image.altText: An SA police car parked on the side of a street with trees on the opposite side

items.title: Magnitude-5.5 earthquake 'morning wake-up call' to remote community

children.content: A remote community in the Anangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara (APY) Lands have woken up to rattling shelves and swinging lights caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake, Geoscience Australia says.

image.altText: A red dirt football oval next to a grassed football oval and a small community with a mountain range in the background

items.title: After initial backlash, how do locals feel about the 'Blue Blob' now?

children.content: Cast in Blue has been the subject of criticism, ridicule and even vandalism over the past 10 months, but Mount Gambier's community has begun to embrace it.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing a black and white top and glasses stands next to a blue sculpture with her arm wrapped around its trunk.

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing a black and white top and glasses stands next to a blue sculpture with her arm wrapped around its trunk.

items.title: Fuel prices force commute changes, as food becomes 'privilege' for some

children.content: A growing number of Australians are factoring rising fuel costs into a tightening budget, including seeking cheaper alternative transport, with one charity warning the flow on effects of the crisis are causing household stress for many families.

image.altText: A woman looks serious, leans an arm on the bonnet of a SUV parked in a driveway

items.title: Tech starts to eat itself as AI reshapes workforce

children.content: The wave of change from artificial intelligence rolling across the corporate landscape is going largely unnoticed as attention understandably shifts to an escalating war.

image.altText: A silhouette of a person looking at a phone.

items.title: Fuel thieves targeting NSW truckers as they sleep

children.content: The owner of a trucking company in Western Sydney says most cases of fuel theft have occurred when his drivers have parked on the side of the road for the night.

image.altText: Hand holding petrol cap with cap inside tank

items.title: Government focused on supply, 'not considering' fuel rationing: Rowland

children.content: Attorney-General Michelle Rowland says the government is focused on securing supply as she insists fuel rationing is not being considered right now. Follow live.

image.altText: Michelle Rowland press conference

items.title: Canberra woman in an aged care 'black hole' sees no way out

children.content: After being stuck in a hospital in Canberra for 19 months, paraplegic 84-year-old Mary Wansey has lost hope of ever finding a place in aged care.

gallery.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

items.title: Man sent home from hospital with '10/10' pain ends up losing chunk of leg

children.content: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.

items.title: Israeli settler violence in the West Bank deepens against backdrop of Iran war

children.content: Under the cover of regional conflict, Israelis who have established illegal settlements and outposts in the West Bank become emboldened to take over more land as world attention remains on the war in Iran.

image.altText: Mourners sit outside a hospital morgue in the West Bank.

items.title: Pro-climate son-in-law to Rupert Murdoch backs Farrer independent

children.content: Backers of Farrer independent candidate Michelle Milthorpe are celebrating after securing the support of a prominent pro-climate farmer who is also the son-in-law of media mogul Rupert Murdoch.

gallery.altText: Michelle Milthorpe talks to two people with their backs to the camera in the bistro of a pub.

thumbnail.altText: Michelle Milthorpe talks to two people with their backs to the camera in the bistro of a pub.

items.title: ASX surges 2pc after modest gains on Wall St as Iran deadline looms

children.content: The Australian surges out of the gates after a four-day long weekend, as traders appear to look through the ongoing uncertainty in the Middle East. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Giant screen on the wall of the ASX, with large green numbers, showing the price of shares which have risen.

items.title: CFA firefighters 'really angry, frustrated' at Easter break-in

children.content: CFA firefighters are "gutted" after thousands of dollars of equipment and fuel were stolen from Terip Terip station over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: A woman in her 30s looks to her left. She is wearing a blue singlet with black dust and soot on it.

children.content: For Heywire winner Zein, her family is remembered in the fragments she remembers of Syria.

items.title: Hidden costs mean many comedians make 'zero profit' from festival runs

children.content: Doing a run of shows at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival is the pinnacle for a lot of Australian comedians, but the festival's tens of thousands of punters are rarely privy to the behind-the-scenes costs.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

items.title: How China uses ferries and fishing boats to pressure Taiwan

children.content: China has quietly mobilised thousands of fishing boats twice in recent months to form floating barriers at least 300 kilometres long.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic features fishing vessels, a target and maps featuring the flags of China and Taiwan.

items.title: A luxury beef giant's cattle died of thirst. Regulators were told later

children.content: The lack of investigation into deaths at the $700 million beef business has been described as "completely unacceptable" by a legal expert who argued industry had captured government regulators.

image.altText: A herd of AACo's wagyu heifers at Westholme station in Queensland.

items.title: Owner of dog that mauled young girl charged over attack

children.content: The owner of a dog that savaged a girl in a Perth suburb, leaving her with serious facial injuries, is charged under Western Australia's Dog Act.

image.altText: A residential street in the Perth suburb of Hillarys on a sunny day, with houses on both sides of the road.

items.title: Britain has become a broken, poverty-riddled 'living nightmare'

children.content: London. It's the epicentre of Britain but without it the UK's GDP is equivalent to the poorest state in the US. And one fifth of the total population is living in poverty.

items.title: 'Just a human being': Day has sympathy for 'hero' Woods over addiction

children.content: Australian number two Jason Day says Tiger Woods's struggles are a reminder that anyone can struggle with addiction, but admits it was "selfish" for the golf great to get behind the wheel.

image.altText: GOlfers Jason Day and Tiger Woods shake hands at the Masters in 2024.

items.title: Wrecked boats spill styrofoam, chemicals into Great Barrier Reef for months

children.content: The vessel 7 Wonder has sat wrecked at Rose Bay in north Queensland for more than two months. Locals say they have been picking up rubbish from the boat daily, but maritime authorities say local salvage contractors are "stretched".

image.altText: A big boat washed up on rocks

items.title: Residents worry link between minister and wind farm project could dilute advocacy

children.content: Despite the links being declared, some locals worry politicians with a financial stake in a planned development in Tasmania's north will have little sympathy for their concerns about the project.

items.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after cyclone leaves animals strewn along beaches

children.content: As sharks swarm in the aftermath, there are fears Cyclone Narelle might be a climate disaster for the world-famous Ningaloo Reef.

image.altText: A dead dolphin on a beach, with debris washed up behind it.

items.title: Boy dies after crash between e-scooter and car south of Perth

children.content: An 11-year-old boy dies in hospital after the e-scooter he was riding collided with a car in Mandurah, with the 46-year-old driver of the car assisting police with their inquiries.

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is increasingly confident the system will move towards the Queensland coast later in the week, potentially crossing Cape York Peninsula.
